page_content: ENEB455 Advanced FPGA System Design using Verilog for Embedded Systems (3 Credits) A project-oriented course on digital system design using Verilog hardware description language (HDL) in an industry-standard design environment appropriate for embedded systems. Students will implement real-world designs in ﬁeld programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) as well as test and optimize the FPGA. Students will also work in teams on multiple, medium- scale digital system design projects and make oral presentations and written reports. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in ENEB344 and ENEB340; and permission from the Cyber-Physical Systems Engineering program. Restriction: Must be in the Cyber-Physical Systems Engineering program.
